title: Freedom Score
subtitle: Data by [Freedom House](#dod:freedom-house). The score captures the extent of free and fair elections, political pluralism and participation, a functioning government, freedom of expression and association, the rule of law, and personal autonomy. Higher scores indicate more freedom.
caption:
Freedom Score

Data by Freedom House. The score captures the extent of free and fair elections, political pluralism and participation, a functioning government, freedom of expression and association, the rule of law, and personal autonomy. Higher scores indicate more freedom.

This choropleth world map displays Freedom Scores for 2025, where higher values indicate greater freedom. The legend ranges from 0 to 101, with color bands marking intervals at 20, 40, 60, 80, and 101. The darkest blue band represents the highest scores, approaching the maximum of 101. This band includes the United States, Canada, Australia, Japan, and the majority of Western European nations such as France, Germany, and the United Kingdom. In South America, Argentina and Uruguay also appear in this darkest category. A slightly lighter blue band, corresponding to scores roughly between 80 and 100, covers countries like Brazil, India, and South Africa. Mid-range scores, represented by teal and light green shades falling between 40 and 80, are visible across much of Eastern Europe, parts of Southeast Asia including Indonesia, and scattered nations in Africa and South America. The lightest yellow shades, indicating scores below 40, dominate the visual landscape of North Africa, the Middle East, and Central Asia, including Russia and China. Greenland is marked with diagonal hatching, indicating no data is available for that territory. The visual distribution shows a concentration of the highest scores in North America, Western Europe, and Oceania, while the lowest scores are clustered across North Africa, the Middle East, and parts of Eurasia.
